title: Module 10: Planning & Implementation Intentions
learning_objective: Check readiness before moving to planning; Elicit the patient's ideas for plans first; Use if-then implementation intentions; Link plans to existing routines and check confidence
scenario_context: Learn to help patients create specific, realistic action plans using implementation intentions

persona_config.name: That
persona_config.background: Intends to take action soon and may have already taken some small steps toward change.

plan_1.persona_text: I've been thinking about it more, and I think I'm ready to start making some changes with my eating and activity. I want to do something.

choice_points.option_text: Great! Here's what you need to do: eat 1500 calories a day and exercise for 45 minutes, five times a week.

feedback.immediate: This jumps immediately to a practitioner-generated plan without exploring the patient's ideas.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: It sounds like you're feeling ready to take some steps. Would you like to think about what a realistic first step might look like for you?

feedback.immediate: Perfect - acknowledges readiness, asks permission, and invites patient's ideas.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: You say you're ready, but are you really? You've said that before.

feedback.immediate: This undermines the patient's expressed readiness rather than supporting it.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

plan_2a.persona_text: That sounds really intense. I don't think I can do all that right away.

choice_points.option_text: If you're serious about losing weight, this is what it takes. You have to commit.

feedback.immediate: This creates pressure and treats planning as fixed rather than collaborative.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: That does sound like a lot. What would feel like a realistic place for you to start?

feedback.immediate: Good recovery - validates concern and returns to patient-generated planning.
feedback.learning_note: Validation acknowledges the patient's experience without judgment.

plan_2b.persona_text: Maybe I could start by swapping my afternoon snack - I usually have chips - for something healthier? And maybe try to walk for 10 minutes a few times a week?

choice_points.option_text: Those are thoughtful, specific ideas that you came up with. Let's get more specific - would it be okay to think about when and how these would happen?

feedback.immediate: Excellent - affirms patient's ideas and seeks permission to make plans concrete.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: That's a good start, but you'll need to do more than that to see real results.

feedback.immediate: This devalues what the patient proposed as insufficient.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: Let's make it every day, not just a few times a week. You should commit to daily walking.

feedback.immediate: This overrides the patient's realistic assessment of what they can commit to.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

plan_2c.persona_text: Well, when you put it like that, I'm not sure I'm ready after all.

choice_points.option_text: See, I knew you weren't really ready. Let's just wait until you're more motivated.

feedback.immediate: This damages the relationship and abandons the planning process.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: It sounds like I pushed too hard there. What would feel right for you to try?

feedback.immediate: Good - practitioner takes responsibility for the discord and returns to collaboration.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

plan_3a.persona_text: Sure, that would help. I usually snack around 3pm at my desk when I hit that afternoon slump.

choice_points.option_text: So if it's 3pm and you're at your desk feeling that slump, what could you do instead of reaching for chips?

feedback.immediate: Perfect - uses if-then format to create a concrete implementation intention.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: You should keep healthy snacks at your desk. I recommend carrot sticks or almonds.

feedback.immediate: This gives advice without eliciting the patient's own ideas first.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

plan_4a.persona_text: I could grab the apple I bring for lunch but never eat? Or maybe take a quick walk around the office instead of snacking?

choice_points.option_text: Those are both smart alternatives - using something you already have, or movement instead of food. Which feels most doable to you?

feedback.immediate: Good - affirms both options and lets patient choose what fits best.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: Walking is better than the apple because you need the exercise more than changing your snack.

feedback.immediate: This takes over decision-making instead of letting the patient choose.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

plan_5a.persona_text: I think the apple. The walk might be too much to add right now, but eating the apple I already have - that seems easy.

choice_points.option_text: So your plan is: if it's 3pm and I'm at my desk feeling the afternoon slump, then I'll eat my apple instead of chips. How confident do you feel about doing that?

feedback.immediate: Excellent - restates the if-then plan clearly and checks confidence before finalizing.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: Good. Now let's plan your walking schedule in detail too.

feedback.immediate: This moves to another change without completing the first or checking if patient wants to continue.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

plan_6a.persona_text: Pretty confident, maybe an 8. I always have an apple with me, and my desk is private so no one will notice.

choice_points.option_text: An 8 is solid. What would make it easier to remember to do this, especially on tough days?

feedback.immediate: Good - reinforces confidence and prepares for challenges.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: Perfect! So you'll do this every day without fail starting tomorrow.

feedback.immediate: This makes the plan inflexible and pressures absolute adherence.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

plan_7a.persona_text: Maybe I could put the apple right on my keyboard in the morning? That way I'd see it and remember. And if I have a really bad day and still want chips, that's okay - I can try again the next day.

choice_points.option_text: You're thinking ahead about both a reminder and how to be flexible with yourself. That's a really thoughtful approach. What about the walking - did you want to make a plan for that too, or start with just the snack swap?

feedback.immediate: Perfect - affirms planning and lets patient decide scope without pressure.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: No, you can't have chips even on bad days. You have to stick to the plan.

feedback.immediate: This creates unrealistic expectations and treats plans as fixed.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

plan_end_good.persona_text: Let me try the snack thing for a couple weeks and see how it goes. Once that's a habit, I'll think about adding the walking. One thing at a time feels more realistic.

plan_end_poor.persona_text: This all feels like too much pressure. I don't think I'm ready for this after all.
